basic_details.job_title: Central Sanskrit University Recruitment 2026: 90 Teaching Posts, Apply Online Before 10 July
basic_details.short_information: Central Sanskrit University (CSU), Delhi has released dated 10.06.2026, inviting online applications for 90 Teaching and Other Academic posts across its three campuses — Vedvyas (Himachal Pradesh), Shri Raghunath Kirti (Uttarakhand), and Ekalavya (Tripura). Online applications can be submitted from 10 June 2026 to 10 July 2026 (11:30 PM) through the CU Chayan Portal at curec.samarth.ac.in.

important_dates.hard copy submission deadline: Within 15 days from the online application closing date

application_fee.details: The application fee for CSU Recruitment 2026 depends on the post level (10 or 13A) and the candidate's category, and must be paid only through online mode. Here is the complete fee structure: | It's important to note that fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances, and a separate application along with the prescribed fee is required for each subject/post applied for. One registration is sufficient to apply for multiple posts, but separate fee payment is mandatory for each subject.

qualification.details: For candidates wondering about the educational qualifications and age limits for these posts, CSU has specified that the detailed eligibility for each post is given separately in Annexure-I of the official notification. Qualifications and other eligibility criteria are determined as per the norms of the UGC, NCTE, NCISM, and the Statutes/Ordinances of CSU, including amendments made from time to time. | The age limit and relaxations are governed by applicable UGC Regulations and Government of India norms, along with the Statutes/Ordinances of CSU. The criteria date for determining eligibility in every respect — including qualifications, NET/Ph.D., teaching/research experience, and publications — is the last date of online application submission, i.e., 10 July 2026. Candidates should refer to Annexure-I of the official notification for post-wise qualification details.

salary_details.details: Candidates curious about the pay structure can refer to the Pay Level (as per 7th CPC) mentioned against each post in the official notification. | Associate Professor posts (Part A) are placed at Pay Level-13A. Assistant Professor, Assistant Director, and Assistant Librarian posts (Part B) are placed at Pay Level-10. | The University has not specified exact in-hand salary figures in the notification; candidates can refer to the standard 7th Pay Commission pay matrix corresponding to Level-10 and Level-13A for indicative pay ranges.

how_to_apply: Applicants who want to know the exact application steps can follow this simple process, which is entirely online except for the final hard-copy submission.
Visit the CU Chayan Portal at curec.samarth.ac.in or access it via www.sanskrit.nic.in under "Recruitment/Notification."
Register yourself on the portal to generate a registration number.
Select the post/subject you wish to apply for and fill in the online application form.
Upload clear, high-quality scanned copies of all required documents, certificates, and publications.
Pay the prescribed application/examination fee online based on your category and post level.
Submit the application before 10 July 2026, 11:30 PM IST (no correction window is available after submission).
Take a printout of the application and fee receipt, sign it, attach self-attested document copies, and superscribe the envelope with the Advertisement Number and post/subject applied for.

Send the hard copy by registered post to "The Registrar, Central Sanskrit University, 56-57, Institutional Area, Janakpuri, New Delhi-110058" so it reaches within 15 days from the online application closing date.
Candidates already employed in Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies must additionally submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) and, where applicable, a Vigilance Clearance Certificate from their employer.

content_sections.heading: Central Sanskrit University Recruitment 2026 - Quick Summary
content_sections.text: Central Sanskrit University is recruiting 90 Teaching posts including Associate Professor (Level-13A) & Assistant Professor with Ph.D pass qualification, last date 10 July 2026. Download Central Sanskrit University Teaching Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

rows.details: Associate Professor (Level-13A) & Assistant Professor / Other Academic (Level-10)
Within 15 days from last date of online application

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for CSU Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply should keep the following timeline in mind, as the University has clearly stated that the online application link will be disabled after the closing date. The criteria date for determining a candidate's eligibility in all respects is the last date of submission of the online application , meaning all qualifications, NET/Ph.D., experience, and publications must be completed by 10 July 2026.

rows.date: Within 15 days from the online application closing date

content_sections.heading: CSU Vacancy Details 2026: Post-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: CSU has divided the 90 vacancies into two parts — Part A covers Associate Professor posts at Pay Level-13A, while Part B covers Assistant Professor and other academic posts at Pay Level-10. Below is the complete post-wise vacancy distribution. *One PwBD (Category-e) vacancy is also earmarked within Part A. Adding both parts, the grand total stands at 90 vacancies (36 UR, 14 SC, 7 ST, 24 OBC, 9 EWS) , with 7 posts reserved for PwBD candidates across categories (a), (b), and (c). Ekalavya Campus – Vill. Sipai Para, P.O. Lembucherra, West Tripura – 799210 Vedvyas Campus – Balahar, Kangra, Himachal Pradesh – 177108 Shri Raghunath Kirti Campus – Devprayag, Pauri Garhwal, Uttarakhand – 249301

tables.caption: Part A: Associate Professor Posts (Level-13A) – Total 23 Vacancies
Part B: Assistant Professor & Other Academic Posts (Level-10) – Total 67 Vacancies

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for CSU Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: For candidates wondering about the educational qualifications and age limits for these posts, CSU has specified that the detailed eligibility for each post is given separately in Annexure-I of the official notification. Qualifications and other eligibility criteria are determined as per the norms of the UGC, NCTE, NCISM, and the Statutes/Ordinances of CSU , including amendments made from time to time. The age limit and relaxations are governed by applicable UGC Regulations and Government of India norms, along with the Statutes/Ordinances of CSU. The criteria date for determining eligibility in every respect — including qualifications, NET/Ph.D., teaching/research experience, and publications — is the last date of online application submission, i.e., 10 July 2026 . Candidates should refer to Annexure-I of the official notification for post-wise qualification details.

content_sections.heading: CSU Application Fee 2026: Category-wise Details
content_sections.text: The application fee for CSU Recruitment 2026 depends on the post level (10 or 13A) and the candidate's category, and must be paid only through online mode . Here is the complete fee structure: It's important to note that fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances , and a separate application along with the prescribed fee is required for each subject/post applied for . One registration is sufficient to apply for multiple posts, but separate fee payment is mandatory for each subject.

content_sections.heading: CSU Selection Process 2026: Step-by-Step
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering how the selection will be conducted can note that the process differs slightly for Associate Professor and Assistant Professor posts, though both involve shortlisting, assessment, and interview stages. Step 1 – Online Application & Scrutiny: Candidates apply online and upload documents; applications are provisionally scrutinized for eligibility. Step 2 – Written Test (for Assistant Professor / Level-10 posts only): An OMR-based test with Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) and a Descriptive Test assessing Research Aptitude and Writing Skills. If eligible candidates for a subject are fewer than 20, the written test may be dispensed with. Step 3 – No Written Test for Associate Professor (Level-13A): These posts go directly to shortlisting based on Video Presentation, Research Skill assessment, and PPT presentation. Step 4 – Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates undergo verification of original certificates related to age, qualification, experience, and category. Step 5 – Face-to-Face Interview: The final stage for all shortlisted candidates across both Level-10 and Level-13A posts. Detailed information on the selection procedure, shortlisting criteria, syllabus, and weightage will be announced separately on the CSU website (www.sanskrit.nic.in). Candidates can choose any one examination centre from Delhi, Mumbai/Pune, Kolkata, or Bengaluru , regardless of which campus they have applied for. Those applying for multiple posts are advised to select only one city to avoid scheduling conflicts. To help candidates applying for multiple subjects avoid clashes, CSU has grouped the subjects into three schedules. Exams within the same schedule are held simultaneously, so a candidate can appear for only one subject per schedule.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for CSU Recruitment 2026 Online
content_sections.text: Applicants who want to know the exact application steps can follow this simple process, which is entirely online except for the final hard-copy submission. Candidates already employed in Govt./PSU/Autonomous Bodies must additionally submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) and, where applicable, a Vigilance Clearance Certificate from their employer.

content_sections.heading: Salary and Pay Level for CSU Posts
content_sections.text: Candidates curious about the pay structure can refer to the Pay Level (as per 7th CPC) mentioned against each post in the official notification. Associate Professor posts (Part A) are placed at Pay Level-13A . Assistant Professor, Assistant Director, and Assistant Librarian posts (Part B) are placed at Pay Level-10 . The University has not specified exact in-hand salary figures in the notification; candidates can refer to the standard 7th Pay Commission pay matrix corresponding to Level-10 and Level-13A for indicative pay ranges.

content_sections.heading: Document Requirements & Caste/Category Certificates
content_sections.text: Reserved category candidates often ask which certificates are mandatory — CSU requires self-attested copies of the Caste/Class Certificate and, for PwBD candidates, a Medical Certificate from the Competent Authority in the Government of India prescribed format. Candidates failing to submit valid category certificates will not be considered for that reservation and will instead be treated as Unreserved (UR) candidates.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying
content_sections.text: Many applicants lose their candidature due to avoidable errors during the CSU application process. Keep the following points in mind to ensure a smooth application:

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for CSU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process started on 10 June 2026 and will close on 10 July 2026 at 11:30 PM IST, after which the application link will be disabled.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in CSU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 90 vacancies are available - 23 for Associate Professor (Level-13A) posts and 67 for Assistant Professor/Other Academic (Level-10) posts.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for CSU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For Level-10 posts, the fee is ₹1500 for UR/OBC/EWS and ₹500 for SC/ST/Women/PwBD/Transgender. For Level-13A posts, it is ₹2000 for UR/OBC/EWS and ₹1000 for SC/ST/Women/PwBD/Transgender.

faq.question: Q4. How can I apply for CSU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online through the CU Chayan Portal at curec.samarth.ac.in, followed by sending a signed hard copy with documents to the Registrar, CSU, within 15 days of the online deadline.

faq.question: Q5. Will there be a written exam for these posts?
faq.answer: A written exam (OMR-based MCQs plus a Descriptive Test) is conducted only for Assistant Professor (Level-10) posts for shortlisting. No written exam is conducted for Associate Professor (Level-13A) posts.

faq.question: Q6. Which campuses are these posts for?
faq.answer: The posts are for three campuses: Vedvyas Campus (Himachal Pradesh), Shri Raghunath Kirti Campus (Uttarakhand), and Ekalavya Campus (Tripura), and are campus-specific.

faq.question: Q7. Is there a minimum service period after joining?
faq.answer: Yes, selected candidates must serve at their initial campus of posting for a minimum period of 10 years (or as prescribed by the University), and personal transfer requests during this period are normally not entertained.

faq.question: Q8. What examination centres are available?
faq.answer: Candidates can choose from Delhi, Mumbai/Pune, Kolkata, or Bengaluru as their examination centre, irrespective of the campus applied for.

faq.question: Q9. What is the OBC certificate validity requirement?
faq.answer: The NCL-OBC certificate must be issued on or after 01.04.2026; certificates issued before this date will not be considered valid for this advertisement.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I check the official CSU Recruitment notification?
faq.answer: The complete detailed advertisement (No. 04/2026) is available on the official website www.sanskrit.nic.in under the "Recruitment/Notification" section.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for CSU Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process started on 10 June 2026 and will close on 10 July 2026 at 11:30 PM IST, after which the application link will be disabled.

faq.answer: A total of 90 vacancies are available - 23 for Associate Professor (Level-13A) posts and 67 for Assistant Professor/Other Academic (Level-10) posts.
